entities.name: Nephilim Prince of Egypt and Babylon

attributes.role: incarnation of the Supreme God and promised Redeemer

entities.raw_quote: worship of the mighty Nephilim Prince of Egypt and Babylon, as the incarnation of the Supreme God and the promised Redeemer of man.

entities.name: Cush
entities.raw_quote: the restoration of the Sun and Nature worship instituted by Cush.

entities.name: Nimrod

attributes.associated with: Sun/Moon/Stars worship, death and resurrection myth

entities.raw_quote: Tammuz, that is Nimrod, was put to death... because he taught the worship of Sun, Moon and Stars...

entities.name: Isis
entities.raw_quote: she, as Isis, or Rhea, the Goddess Mother, should be identified with the Earth, or with the Moon.

entities.name: Osiris
entities.raw_quote: if he, as Osiris, was identified with the Sun... he was supposed to become re-incarnate as Horus, the son of Isis...

entities.name: Horus
entities.raw_quote: he was supposed to become re-incarnate as Horus, the son of Isis...

entities.name: daimonia / devils
entities.raw_quote: spirits... identical with the daimonia, or devils, of Scripture.

entities.name: Serpent god / Satan
entities.raw_quote: the Pagan god was, from the first, morally identical with the Prince of Evil... 'Satan, that old Serpent'

entities.name: fire (ritual)
entities.raw_quote: the supposed spiritual efficacy of fire... continual fires were kept burning before all the altars of the Sun god

entities.name: Phallus / Yoni

attributes.role: symbol of life and generative power

entities.raw_quote: the Phallus as the manifestation in the animal world of the life and generative power... Yoni was a distinctive emblem of the goddess

entities.name: Baal Peor
entities.raw_quote: the worship of Baal Peor... the Israelites succumbed, was the Phallic god of Canaan.

entities.name: Rephaim / Zuzim
entities.raw_quote: the mention of the Rephaim, Zuzim and other Nephilim races... shows that it was then well established

entities.name: priesthood / pontiffs

attributes.associated with: spirit communication, control over unseen powers

entities.raw_quote: the powers of these beings... being wielded by the priesthood... caused them to be regarded as second only to the king

entities.name: image worship / consecration
entities.raw_quote: the spirit of the god was called into the image by the divine (i.e., the priestly) consecration.

entities.name: diviners / enchanters / necromancers

attributes.associated with: Pagan religion, power over unseen world

entities.raw_quote: the powers of these beings... called into play by the diviners, observers of times, enchanters, wizards, sorcerers and necromancers

entities.name: Eucharist (Christian-pagan hybrid)
entities.raw_quote: they introduce it when they bless the Eucharist.

entities.name: brazen serpent (Moses)

attributes.associated with: prefiguring Christ, but misinterpreted by pagans

entities.raw_quote: the brazen serpent was not a symbol of Christ in itself, but of sin crucified by Christ.

summary: This chunk traces the historical development of revived ancient idolatry, linking it to Nephilim influence and spirit communication. It describes how ancestor worship (especially of Cushite monarchs like Nimrod/Osiris) merged with sun/nature worship, leading to a system where spirits—identified as daimonia or evil spirits—were invoked through images, fire rituals, phallic symbols, and necromantic practices. The text explicitly connects these traditions to medieval Christian critiques of paganism and traces their evolution toward serpent/Satanic worship.
key_passages: spirits... identical with the daimonia, or devils, of Scripture.
the spirit of the god was called into the image by the divine (i.e., the priestly) consecration.
Tammuz, that is Nimrod, was put to death... because he taught the worship of Sun, Moon and Stars...
the powers of these beings... being wielded by the priesthood... caused them to be regarded as second only to the king himself
the Pagan god was, from the first, morally identical with the Prince of Evil.
